plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-587 RP19.P02.M06.S13-RP19.P03.M05.S06 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-587 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP19 p02 closeout p03 foundation: the P02 Synthetic Fixture Set tail (the ten remaining rows), the full P02 Test And Golden Case Set, Hermes Evidence Packet, Claude Review Packet, and Closeout And Next Handoff cycles, the full P03 Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition, Primary Implementation Slice, and Secondary Workflow Slice cycles, and the P03 Permission And Audit Binding head (the first six rows) as eleven descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H19), Claude review packet (C19), and CP00-588 handoff, without opening client portal, secure link, client review, or watermark runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-587 is a descriptor-only RP19 Client Portal closeout pack (150 units, RP19.P02.M06.S13-RP19.P03.M05.S06, risk C) that passes independent read-only review. Scope and distributions are consistent across closeout-pack-plan.json (packs[0], ledger sha 13c7ed65...), contracts/client-portal-core-contract.json (current_pack=CP00-587), the registry/service/validators, the manifest delivery_counts, README, and the validator-backed node:test suite. The pack does not over-claim readiness: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review; the production_ready_flag is only a descriptor-verification marker, supported by command-evidence (all listed commands exit 0) and by static inspection of validateClientPortalCoreCp587Coverage / ...FoundationDescriptor. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold (freeze + no_write_attestation flags all false; tests assert descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false). Authority boundary is intact: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob) and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation). Review-hardening holds: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1228-byte stdout), forbidden_review_evidence enumerated, no invalid attempts for this pack, and the manifest contains no pre-fabricated review verdict. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false). Two P3 informational observations only; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: CP587 descriptor validator lacks row-type-specific no-leak assertions for permission/audit rows
findings.details: validateClientPortalCoreCp587P02CloseoutP03FoundationDescriptor asserts only the generic per-row guards (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false). Unlike the CP583/CP584 validators (which add row-type-specific assertions such as permission_baseline_note.permission_decision_detail_included=false and audit_baseline_note.audit_event_body_included=false), the new CP587 security_audit rows (Permission precheck/annotation, Audit hint precheck/annotation) carry no specialized leak assertion because their row keys do not match the inherited CP583 ROW_EXTRAS. The no-leak posture is still declared via required_no_leak_guards and enforced at the descriptor result level by freezeCp587Result (writes_permission_decision=false, appends_audit_trace=false, exposes_permission_decision_detail=false, exposes_audit_event_body=false, evaluates_runtime_permission=false), which the model.test.js / validator suite verifies. No actual leak path exists; this is a defense-in-depth symmetry gap only.
findings.recommendation: Optionally, in a future RP19 pack, add row-level assertions for permission/audit rows for parity with CP583/CP584. Not required for this descriptor-only pack.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Result-level attestation plus passing tests cover the no-leak posture; does not affect the descriptor-only production_ready_flag and does not block closeout.

findings.title: Manifest per-unit evidence_refs forward-reference post-review artifacts not yet present
findings.details: Each unit's evidence_refs lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-587/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in that directory. This is consistent with the documented hardened_review_sequence (read_only_claude_run -> receipt_normalization -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation -> commit) and command-evidence notes that those gated artifacts are produced from the accepted review and recorded before commit. Importantly, the manifest does not embed any pre-decided review verdict, so reviewer independence is preserved.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are generated and present before the closeout commit, as the sequence specifies.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected staging behavior; informational. Verified no pre-fabricated verdict is present. Does not block closeout.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-587 client portal core p02 closeout p03 foundation descriptor pack
